summary: We recommend accepting ADR 0001 rubric-calibration as the v0.1.0 calibration baseline because the D3 confusion matrix on the 40-pair seed set is perfect TP=20 FN=0 TN=20 FP=0 and every rubric dimension fires in isolation across the failing fixture prefixes. The tradeoff against immediately ratcheting the floor to 4.0 is that the seed set has no pairs in the 3.5 to 4.4 band so the ratchet would silently pass without testing the transition; the ADR defers the ratchet to B5 and names the 5 naturalistic pairs needed first. The ADR also recommends wiring scripts eval sh into CI as a soft gate so a future verifier edit that silently breaks a dimension flips the matrix from perfect to red.

claims.statement: ADR 0001 records the D3 confusion matrix on canonical floor 3.5 as TP=20 FN=0 TN=20 FP=0 with accuracy precision and recall all equal to 1.000 because the ADR cites the evals results 2026-05-22 json file which the D3 tick produced.

citations.title: ADR 0001 baseline table records the matrix and metrics verbatim
evals results 2026-05-22 json the source of the matrix and metrics

claims.caveats: Perfect classification on a hand-curated seed set is uninformative in isolation; the ADR explicitly calls this out and recommends growing D1 with naturalistic pairs in the calibration blind spot.

claims.statement: ADR 0001 records that every rubric dimension fires in isolation across the failing fixture prefix buckets a* clarity b* citation_quality c* confidence_calibration d* coverage e* decision_usefulness and f* combined because the D2 MANIFEST attributes each failing pair to a single dimension and every pair scored below 3.5.

citations.title: ADR 0001 per-dimension firing table maps each prefix bucket to its target dimension
D2 MANIFEST records per-pair dimension attribution and observed scores

claims.caveats: The combined f* pairs exercise multiple dimensions simultaneously so they cannot attribute a regression to a single dimension if D3 later flips one of them to false positive; this is acceptable because the a* through e* buckets provide single-dimension attribution.

claims.statement: ADR 0001 explicitly defers the floor ratchet to 4.0 to outcome B5 and names the calibration shoulder needed first which is 5 additional naturalistic D1 pairs likely to score in the 3.5 to 4.0 band so the ratchet exercises the transition rather than silently passing.

citations.title: ADR 0001 Decision section 4 defers the ratchet to B5 and names the shoulder requirement
QUEUE md B5 row depends on D3 and is now unblocked by this tick

claims.caveats: If a future operator-present tick decides the ratchet is more important than the shoulder the ADR can be superseded by ADR 0002; that is the standard ADR succession pattern.

open_questions: Should ADR 0001 also require D5 staleness and D6 URL liveness to land before B5 since those two checks are the asymmetric tests that the perfect matrix cannot currently catch?
methodology_notes: Read evals results 2026-05-22 json and evals pairs failing MANIFEST md to extract the matrix and per-dimension firing data then authored the ADR with explicit Status Date and Decision sections and a Consequences section that names the calibration blind spot. The ADR cross-references B5 D5 D6 and E3 as downstream consumers of this baseline.
